  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

native.switch

Select the first source whose predicate is true in a list. If the second argument is a getter, the source will be dynamically created.

Type:

(?id : string?, ?track_sensitive : bool,
 [{bool} * source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c)]) ->
source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • track_sensitive (of type bool, which defaults to true): Re-select only on end of tracks.
  • (unlabeled) (of type [{bool} * source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c)]): Sources with the predicate telling when they can be played.

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • set (of type (source(audio=?A, video=?B, midi=?C)) -> unit): Set the source.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

notify_metadata

Use notify to display metadata info.

Type:

(?urgency : string, ?icon : string, ?time : int,
 ?display : (([string * string]) -> string),
 ?title : string, 'b
 .{on_metadata : (((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> 'a)
 }) -> 'a

Arguments:

  • urgency (of type string, which defaults to "low"): Urgency (low|normal|critical).
  • icon (of type string, which defaults to "stock_smiley-22"): Icon filename or stock icon to display.
  • time (of type int, which defaults to 3000): Timeout in milliseconds.
  • display (of type ([string * string]) -> string, which defaults to <fun>): Function used to display a metadata packet.
  • title (of type string, which defaults to "Liquidsoap: new track"): Title of the notification message.
  • (unlabeled) (of type 'b.{on_metadata : (((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> 'a) })

osd_metadata

Use X On Screen Display to display metadata info.

Type:

(?color : string, ?position : string, ?font : string,
 ?display : (([string * string]) -> string), 'b
 .{on_metadata : (((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> 'a)
 }) -> 'a

Arguments:

  • color (of type string, which defaults to "green"): Color of the text.
  • position (of type string, which defaults to "top"): Position of the text (top|middle|bottom).
  • font (of type string, which defaults to "-*-courier-*-r-*-*-*-240-*-*-*-*-*-*"): Font used (xfontsel is your friend…)
  • display (of type ([string * string]) -> string, which defaults to <fun>): Function used to display a metadata packet.
  • (unlabeled) (of type 'b.{on_metadata : (((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> 'a) })

skipper

Regularly skip tracks from a source (useful for testing skipping).

Type:

(?every : {float}, source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c)
 .{skip : () -> unit, time : (() -> float)}) ->
source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c)

Arguments:

  • every (of type {float}, which defaults to 5.): How often to skip tracks.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video='b, midi='c) .{skip : () -> unit, time : (() -> float)}): The stream.

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

smooth_add

Mixes two streams, with faded transitions between the state when only the normal stream is available and when the special stream gets added on top of it.

Type:

(?duration : float, ?p : {float},
 normal : source(audio=pcm('a), video=internal('b),
 midi=internal('c)), special : source(audio=pcm('a),
 video=internal('b), midi=internal('c))) ->
source(audio=pcm('a), video=internal('b),
midi=internal('c))

Arguments:

  • duration (of type float, which defaults to 1.): Duration of the fade in seconds.
  • p (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): Portion of amplitude of the normal source in the mix.
  • normal (of type source(audio=pcm('a), video=internal('b), midi=internal('c))): The normal source, which could be called the carrier too.
  • special (of type source(audio=pcm('a), video=internal('b), midi=internal('c))): The special source.

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

Source / Video Processing

input.external.ffmpeg

Input from ffmpeg.

Type:

(?id : string, ?show_command : bool, ?restart : bool,
 ?restart_on_error : bool, ?buffer : float, ?max : float,
 string) -> source(audio=pcm(stereo), video=yuva420p('a),
midi=none)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string, which defaults to "input.external.ffmpeg")
  • show_command (of type bool, which defaults to false)
  • restart (of type bool, which defaults to true): restart on exit.
  • restart_on_error (of type bool, which defaults to false): restart on exit with error.
  • buffer (of type float, which defaults to 0.2): Duration of the pre-buffered data.
  • max (of type float, which defaults to 10.): Maximum duration of the buffered data.
  • (unlabeled) (of type string): ffmpeg options specifying the input

video.external.testsrc

ffmpeg’s test source video (useful for testing and debugging).

Type:

(?id : string, ?restart : bool, ?restart_on_error : bool,
 ?buffer : float, ?max : float, ?framerate : int) ->
source(audio=pcm(stereo), video=yuva420p('a), midi=none)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string, which defaults to "video.external.testsrc")
  • restart (of type bool, which defaults to true): restart on exit.
  • restart_on_error (of type bool, which defaults to false): restart on exit with error.
  • buffer (of type float, which defaults to 0.2): Duration of the pre-buffered data.
  • max (of type float, which defaults to 10.): Maximum duration of the buffered data.
  • framerate (of type int, which defaults to 0)

video.frei0r._3_point_color_balance

Adjust color balance with 3 color points (by Maksim Golovkin).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?black_color : int, ?gray_color : int,
 ?source_image_on_left_side : bool,
 ?split_preview : bool, ?white_color : int,
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • black_color (of type int, which defaults to 0): Black color.
  • gray_color (of type int, which defaults to 8355711): Gray color.
  • source_image_on_left_side (of type bool, which defaults to true): Source image on left side.
  • split_preview (of type bool, which defaults to true): Split privew.
  • white_color (of type int, which defaults to 16777215): White color.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r._3dflippo

Frame rotation in 3d-space (by c.e. prelz AS FLUIDO <fluido(at)fluido.as>).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?center_position : {float},
 ?center_position : {float}, ?don't_blank_mask : bool,
 ?fill_with_image_or_black : bool,
 ?invert_rotation_assignment : bool,
 ?x_axis_rotation : {float},
 ?x_axis_rotation_rate : {float},
 ?y_axis_rotation : {float},
 ?y_axis_rotation_rate : {float},
 ?z_axis_rotation : {float},
 ?z_axis_rotation_rate : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • center_position (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Position of the center of rotation on the X axis.
  • center_position (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Position of the center of rotation on the Y axis.
  • don't_blank_mask (of type bool, which defaults to false): Mask for frame transposition is not blanked, so a trace of old transpositions is maintained.
  • fill_with_image_or_black (of type bool, which defaults to false): If true, pixels that are not transposed are black, otherwise, they are copied with the original.
  • invert_rotation_assignment (of type bool, which defaults to false): If true, when mapping rotation, make inverted (wrong) assignment.
  • x_axis_rotation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation on the X axis.
  • x_axis_rotation_rate (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation rate on the X axis.
  • y_axis_rotation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation on the Y axis.
  • y_axis_rotation_rate (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation rate on the Y axis.
  • z_axis_rotation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation on the Z axis.
  • z_axis_rotation_rate (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Rotation rate on the Z axis.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.alphaspot

Draws simple shapes into the alpha channel (by Marko Cebokli).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?max : {float}, ?min : {float},
 ?operation : {float}, ?position_x : {float},
 ?position_y : {float}, ?shape : {float},

 ?transition_width : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • max (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): .
  • min (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): .
  • operation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): .
  • position_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): .
  • position_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): .
  • shape (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): .
  • size_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.10000000149): .
  • size_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.10000000149): .

  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

video.frei0r.burn

Perform an RGB[A] dodge operation between the pixel sources, using the generalised algorithm: D = saturation of 255 or depletion of 0, of ((255 - A) * 256) / (B + 1) (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->

video.frei0r.c0rners

Four corners geometry engine (by Marko Cebokli).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?alpha_operation : {float},
 ?corner_1_x : {float}, ?corner_1_y : {float},
 ?corner_2_x : {float}, ?corner_2_y : {float},
 ?corner_3_x : {float}, ?corner_3_y : {float},
 ?corner_4_x : {float}, ?corner_4_y : {float},
 ?enable_stretch : bool, ?feather_alpha : {float},
 ?interpolator : {float}, ?stretch_x : {float},
 ?stretch_y : {float}, ?transparent_background : bool,
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • alpha_operation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): .
  • corner_1_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.33333298564): X coordinate of corner 1.
  • corner_1_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.33333298564): Y coordinate of corner 1.
  • corner_2_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666665971279): X coordinate of corner 2.
  • corner_2_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.33333298564): Y coordinate of corner 2.
  • corner_3_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666665971279): X coordinate of corner 3.
  • corner_3_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666665971279): Y coordinate of corner 3.
  • corner_4_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.33333298564): X coordinate of corner 4.
  • corner_4_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666665971279): Y coordinate of corner 4.
  • enable_stretch (of type bool, which defaults to false): Enable stretching.
  • feather_alpha (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.00999999977648): Makes smooth transition into transparent.
  • interpolator (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.166666671634): Quality of interpolation.
  • stretch_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of stretching in X direction.
  • stretch_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of stretching in Y direction.
  • transparent_background (of type bool, which defaults to false): Makes background transparent.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.cairoaffineblend

Composites second input on first input applying user-defined transformation, opacity and blend mode (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?anchor_x : {float}, ?anchor_y : {float},
 ?blend_mode : string, ?opacity : {float},
 ?rotation : {float}, ?x : {float}, ?x_scale : {float},
 ?y : {float}, ?y_scale : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c), source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • anchor_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): X position of rotation center within the second input.
  • anchor_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Y position of rotation center within the second input.
  • blend_mode (of type string, which defaults to "\160]\196f\191U"): Blend mode used to compose image. Accepted values: ‘normal’, ‘add’, ‘saturate’, ‘multiply’, ‘screen’, ‘overlay’, ‘darken’, ‘lighten’, ‘colordodge’, ‘colorburn’, ‘hardlight’, ‘softlight’, ‘difference’, ‘exclusion’, ‘hslhue’, ‘hslsaturation’, ‘hslcolor’, ‘hslluminosity’.
  • opacity (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Opacity of second input.
  • rotation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Rotation of second input, value interperted as range 0 - 360.
  • x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.4): X position of second input, value interperted as range -2width - 3width.
  • x_scale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): X scale of second input, value interperted as range 0 - 5.
  • y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.4): Y position of second input, value interperted as range -2height - 3height.
  • y_scale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): Y scale of second input, value interperted as range 0 - 5.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.cairoblend

Composites second input on the first input with user-defined blend mode and opacity (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?blend_mode : string, ?opacity : {float},
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c),
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • blend_mode (of type string, which defaults to "@\\\198f\191U"): Blend mode used to compose image. Accepted values: ‘normal’, ‘add’, ‘saturate’, ‘multiply’, ‘screen’, ‘overlay’, ‘darken’, ‘lighten’, ‘colordodge’, ‘colorburn’, ‘hardlight’, ‘softlight’, ‘difference’, ‘exclusion’, ‘hslhue’, ‘hslsaturation’, ‘hslcolor’, ‘hslluminosity’.
  • opacity (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Opacity of composited image.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.

  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.cairogradient

Draws a gradient on top of image. Filter is given gradient start and end points, colors and opacities (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?blend_mode : string, ?end_color : int,
 ?end_opacity : {float}, ?end_x : {float},
 ?end_y : {float}, ?offset : {float}, ?pattern : string,
 ?start_color : int, ?start_opacity : {float},
 ?start_x : {float}, ?start_y : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • blend_mode (of type string, which defaults to "\128\\\196f\191U"): Blend mode used to compose gradient on image. Accepted values: ‘normal’, ‘add’, ‘saturate’, ‘multiply’, ‘screen’, ‘overlay’, ‘darken’, ‘lighten’, ‘colordodge’, ‘colorburn’, ‘hardlight’, ‘softlight’, ‘difference’, ‘exclusion’, ‘hslhue’, ‘hslsaturation’, ‘hslcolor’, ‘hslluminosity’.
  • end_color (of type int, which defaults to 16777215): Second color of the gradient.
  • end_opacity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Opacity of the second color of the gradient.
  • end_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): X position of the end point of the gradient.
  • end_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Y position of the end point of the gradient.
  • offset (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Position of first color in the line connecting gradient ends, really useful only for radial gradient.
  • pattern (of type string, which defaults to " \\\196f\191U"): Linear or radial gradient.
  • start_color (of type int, which defaults to 0): First color of the gradient.
  • start_opacity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Opacity of the first color of the gradient.
  • start_x (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): X position of the start point of the gradient.
  • start_y (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Y position of the start point of the gradient.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.cairoimagegrid

Draws a grid of input images (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?columns : {float}, ?rows : {float},
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • columns (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.105263157895): Number of columns in the image grid. Input range 0 - 1 is interpreted as range 1 - 20.
  • rows (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.105263157895): Number of rows in the image grid. Input range 0 - 1 is interpreted as range 1 - 20.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

video.frei0r.cartoon

Cartoonify video, do a form of edge detect (by Dries Pruimboom, Jaromil).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?diffspace : {float},
 ?triplevel : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • diffspace (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.00390625): difference space: a value from 0 to 256 (mapped to [0,1]).
  • triplevel (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): level of trip: mapped to [0,1] asymptotical.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

video.frei0r.color_distance

Calculates the distance between the selected color and the current pixel and uses that value as new pixel value (by Richard Spindler).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?color : int, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

video.frei0r.color_only

Perform a conversion to color only of the source input1 using the hue and saturation values of input2 (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

video.frei0r.coloradj_rgb

Simple color adjustment (by Marko Cebokli).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?action : {float},
 ?alpha_controlled : bool, ?b : {float}, ?g : {float},
 ?keep_luma : bool, ?luma_formula : {float},
 ?r : {float}, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • action (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.333344429731): Type of color adjustment.
  • alpha_controlled (of type bool, which defaults to false): Adjust only areas with nonzero alpha.
  • b (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of blue.
  • g (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of green.
  • keep_luma (of type bool, which defaults to true): Don’t change brightness.
  • luma_formula (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.500025033951): .
  • r (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of red.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

video.frei0r.colorhalftone

Filters image to resemble a halftone print in which tones are represented as variable sized dots (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?cyan_angle : {float},
 ?dot_radius : {float}, ?magenta_angle : {float},
 ?yellow_angle : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • cyan_angle (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.3): Cyan dots angle.
  • dot_radius (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.4): Halftone pattern dot size.
  • magenta_angle (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.45): Magenta dots angle.
  • yellow_angle (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.25): Yellow dots angle.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.

  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.

  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.

  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.colorize

Colorizes image to selected hue, saturation and lightness (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?hue : {float}, ?lightness : {float},
 ?saturation : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • hue (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Color shade of the colorized image.
  • lightness (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Lightness of the colorized image.
  • saturation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Amount of color in the colorized image.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.colortap

Applies a pre-made color effect to image (by Janne Liljeblad).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?table : string, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • table (of type string, which defaults to "\160G\190f\191U"): Lookup table used to filter colors. One of: xpro, sepia, heat, red_green, old_photo, xray, esses, yellow_blue.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

video.frei0r.defish0r

Non rectilinear lens mappings (by Marko Cebokli).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?amount : {float},
 ?aspect_type : {float}, ?defish : bool,
 ?interpolator : {float}, ?manual_aspect : {float},
 ?manual_scale : {float}, ?scaling : {float},
 ?type : {float}, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • amount (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Focal Ratio.
  • aspect_type (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Pixel aspect ratio presets.
  • defish (of type bool, which defaults to false): Fish or Defish.
  • interpolator (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.166666671634): Quality of interpolation.
  • manual_aspect (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Manual Pixel Aspect ratio.
  • manual_scale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Manual Scale.
  • scaling (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666666686535): Scaling method.
  • type (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.666666686535): Mapping function.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,

  • velocity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Changing speed of the plasma signal.

 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->

  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.divide

Perform an RGB[A] divide operation between the pixel sources: input1 is the numerator, input2 the denominator (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.

  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.dodge

Perform an RGB[A] dodge operation between the pixel sources, using the generalised algorithm: D = saturation of 255 or (A * 256) / (256 - B) (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->

video.frei0r.edgeglow

Edgeglow filter (by Salsaman).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?lredscale : {float}, ?lthresh : {float},
 ?lupscale : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • lredscale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): multiplier for downscaling non-edge brightness.
  • lthresh (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): threshold for edge lightening.
  • lupscale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): multiplier for upscaling edge brightness.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

video.frei0r.elastic_scale_filter

This is a frei0r filter which allows one to scale video footage non-linearly (by Matthias Schnoell).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?center : {float},
 ?linear_scale_factor : {float}, ?linear_width : {float},
 ?non_linear_scale_factor : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • center (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): Horizontal center position of the linear area.
  • linear_scale_factor (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.7): Amount how much the linear area is scaled.
  • linear_width (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Width of the linear area.
  • non_linear_scale_factor (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.7125): Amount how much the outer left and outer right areas are scaled non linearly.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.

  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

 ?width45 : {float}, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

  • azimuth (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.375): Light direction.

video.frei0r.glitch0r

Adds glitches and block shifting (by IDENT Software).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?block_height : {float},
 ?color_glitching_intensity : {float},
 ?glitch_frequency : {float}, ?shift_intensity : {float},
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • block_height (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.428571428571): Height range of the block that will be shifted/glitched.
  • color_glitching_intensity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): How intensive should be color distortion.
  • glitch_frequency (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): How frequently the glitch should be applied.
  • shift_intensity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.428571428571): How much we should move blocks when glitching.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.grain_extract

Perform an RGB[A] grain-extract operation between the pixel sources (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.levels

Adjust luminance or color channel intensity (by Maksim Golovkin).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?black_output : {float},
 ?channel : {float}, ?gamma : {float},
 ?histogram_position : {float},
 ?input_black_level : {float},
 ?input_white_level : {float}, ?show_histogram : bool,
 ?white_output : {float}, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • black_output (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Black output.
  • channel (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.3): Channel to adjust levels. 0%=R, 10%=G, 20%=B, 30%=Luma.
  • gamma (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.25): Gamma.
  • histogram_position (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.3): Histogram position. 0%=TL, 10%=TR, 20%=BL, 30%=BR.
  • input_black_level (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Input black level.
  • input_white_level (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Input white level.
  • show_histogram (of type bool, which defaults to true): Show histogram.
  • white_output (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): White output.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.light_graffiti

Creates light graffitis from a video by keeping the brightest spots (by Simon A. Eugster (Granjow)).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?backgroundweight : {float},
 ?blackreference : bool, ?dim : {float},
 ?longalpha : {float}, ?loweroverexposure : {float},
 ?nonlineardim : bool, ?reset : bool,
 ?saturation : {float}, ?sensitivity : {float},
 ?statsbrightness : bool, ?statsdifference : bool,
 ?statsdiffsum : bool, ?thresholdbrightness : {float},
 ?thresholddifference : {float},
 ?thresholddiffsum : {float},
 ?transparentbackground : bool, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • backgroundweight (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Describes how strong the (accumulated) background should shine through.
  • blackreference (of type bool, which defaults to false): Uses black as background image instead of the first frame..
  • dim (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Dimming of the light mask.
  • longalpha (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Alpha value for moving average.
  • loweroverexposure (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Prevents some overexposure if the light source stays steady too long (varying speed).
  • nonlineardim (of type bool, which defaults to false): Nonlinear dimming (may look more natural).
  • reset (of type bool, which defaults to false): Reset filter masks.
  • saturation (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.25): Saturation of lights.
  • sensitivity (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): Sensitivity of the effect for light (higher sensitivity will lead to brighter lights).
  • statsbrightness (of type bool, which defaults to false): Display the brightness and threshold, for adjusting the brightness threshold parameter.
  • statsdifference (of type bool, which defaults to false): Display the background difference and threshold.
  • statsdiffsum (of type bool, which defaults to false): Display the sum of the background difference and the threshold.
  • thresholdbrightness (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.588235294118): Brightness threshold to distinguish between foreground and background.
  • thresholddifference (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Threshold: Difference to background to distinguish between fore- and background.
  • thresholddiffsum (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Threshold for sum of differences. Can in most cases be ignored (set to 0)..
  • transparentbackground (of type bool, which defaults to false): Make the background transparent.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.lighten

Perform a lighten operation between two sources (maximum value of both sources) (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

video.frei0r.lissajous0r

Generates Lissajous0r images (by Martin Bayer).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?ratiox : {float}, ?ratioy : {float}) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • ratiox (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): x-ratio.
  • ratioy (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): y-ratio.

video.frei0r.luminance

Creates a luminance map of the image (by Richard Spindler).

 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

video.frei0r.mask0mate

Creates an square alpha-channel mask (by Richard Spindler).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?blur : {float}, ?bottom : {float},
 ?invert : bool, ?left : {float}, ?right : {float},
 ?top : {float}, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • blur (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Blur the outline of the mask.
  • bottom (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): .
  • invert (of type bool, which defaults to false): Invert the mask, creates a hole in the frame..
  • left (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): .
  • right (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): .
  • top (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.2): .

video.frei0r.medians

Implements several median-type filters (by Marko Cebokli).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?size : {float}, ?type : string,
 source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • size (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.10000000149): Size for ‘var size’ type filter.
  • type (of type string, which defaults to "\240I\190f\191U"): Choose type of median: Cross5, Square3x3, Bilevel, Diamond3x3, Square5x5, Temp3, Temp5, ArceBI, ML3D, ML3dEX, VarSize.
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.

  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.multiply

Perform an RGB[A] multiply operation between the pixel sources (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->

video.frei0r.ndvi_filter

This filter creates a false image from a visible + infrared source (by Brian Matherly).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?color_map : string,
 ?index_calculation : string, ?legend : string,
 ?levels : {float}, ?nir_channel : string,
 ?nir_offset : {float}, ?nir_scale : {float},
 ?vis_offset : {float}, ?vis_scale : {float},
 ?visible_channel : string, source(audio='a,
 video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) -> source(audio='a,
video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • color_map (of type string, which defaults to "h\181\185f\191U"): The color map to use. One of ‘earth’, ‘grayscale’, ‘heat’ or ‘rainbow’..
  • index_calculation (of type string, which defaults to "\232\181\185f\191U"): The index calculation to use. One of ‘ndvi’ or ‘vi’..
  • legend (of type string, which defaults to "\b\182\185f\191U"): Control legend display. One of ‘off’ or ‘bottom’..
  • levels (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.256): The number of color levels to use in the false image (divided by 1000)..
  • nir_channel (of type string, which defaults to "\200\181\185f\191U"): The channel to use for the near-infrared component. One of ‘r’, ‘g’, or ‘b’..
  • nir_offset (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): An offset to be applied to the near-infrared component (mapped to [-100%, 100%]..
  • nir_scale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.1): A scaling factor to be applied to the near-infrared component (divided by 10)..
  • vis_offset (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.5): An offset to be applied to the visible component (mapped to [-100%, 100%]..
  • vis_scale (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.1): A scaling factor to be applied to the visible component (divided by 10)..
  • visible_channel (of type string, which defaults to "\168\181\185f\191U"): The channel to use for the visible component. One of ‘r’, ‘g’, or ‘b’..
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.nervous

Flushes frames in time in a nervous way (by Tannenbaum, Kentaro, Jaromil).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.

video.frei0r.normaliz0r

Normalize (aka histogram stretch, contrast stretch) (by Chungzuwalla (chungzuwalla(at)rling.com)).

Type:

(?id : string?, ?blackpt : int, ?independence : {float},
 ?smoothing : {float}, ?strength : {float},
 ?whitept : int, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c)) -> source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
midi='c)

Arguments:

  • id (of type string?, which defaults to null): Force the value of the source ID.
  • blackpt (of type int, which defaults to 0): Output color to which darkest input color is mapped (default black).
  • independence (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Proportion of independent to linked channel normalization (default 1.0).
  • smoothing (of type {float}, which defaults to 0.): Amount of temporal smoothing of the input range, to reduce flicker (default 0.0).
  • strength (of type {float}, which defaults to 1.): Strength of filter, from no effect to full normalization (default 1.0).
  • whitept (of type int, which defaults to 16777215): Output color to which brightest input color is mapped (default white).
  • (unlabeled) (of type source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c))

Methods:

  • fallible (of type bool): Indicate if a source may fail, i.e. may not be ready to stream.
  • id (of type () -> string): Identifier of the source.
  • is_active (of type () -> bool): true if the source is active, i.e. it is continuously animated by its own clock whenever it is ready. Typically, true for outputs and sources such as input.http.
  • is_ready (of type () -> bool): Indicate if a source is ready to stream. This does not mean that the source is currently streaming, just that its resources are all properly initialized.
  • is_up (of type () -> bool): Indicate that the source can be asked to produce some data at any time. This is true when the source is currently being used or if it could be used at any time, typically inside a switch or fallback.
  • log (of type {level : (() -> int?).{set : ((int) -> unit)}}): Get or set the source’s log level, from 1 to 5.
  • on_leave (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source is not used anymore by another source.
  • on_metadata (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on metadata packets.
  • on_shutdown (of type ((() -> unit)) -> unit): Register a function to be called when source shuts down.
  • on_track (of type ((([string * string]) -> unit)) -> unit): Call a given handler on new tracks.
  • remaining (of type () -> float): Estimation of remaining time in the current track.
  • seek (of type (float) -> float): Seek forward, in seconds (returns the amount of time effectively seeked).
  • self_sync (of type () -> bool): Is the source currently controling its own real-time loop.
  • shutdown (of type () -> unit): Deactivate a source.
  • skip (of type () -> unit): Skip to the next track.
  • time (of type () -> float): Get a source’s time, based on its assigned clock.

video.frei0r.overlay

Perform an RGB[A] overlay operation between the pixel sources, using the generalised algorithm: D = A * (B + (2 * B) * (255 - A)) (by Jean-Sebastien Senecal).

Type:

(?id : string?, source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b),
 midi='c), source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)) ->
source(audio='a, video=yuva420p('b), midi='c)
